claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary audit implementation exists with git-backed and non-git fallback coverage, but has not been stress-tested with real Claude -p agents in the current dirty working tree.

evidence.summary: Git working tree shows 65 modified files and untracked .yolo/demand/, .yolo/stress/ directories, confirming dirty working tree claim.
evidence.why: Verifies the status_quo claim that YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() combines git ls-files output with walkFiles() fallback and excludes allowedRoots (artifact root only).
evidence.why: Core boundary snapshot logic implements desired outcome of covering tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' to get tracked, cached, and other (untracked but not ignored) files.
evidence.why: Implements git-backed detection of tracked and untracked target-project mutations.

evidence.summary: walkFiles() recursively walks directory tree excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache directories.
evidence.why: Provides non-git fallback coverage for projects without version control.

evidence.summary: excludedDir() blocks .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache from file walks.
evidence.why: Exclusions prevent noisy detector churn from build artifacts and dependencies.

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshots before and after agent execution, then diffs to detect violations.
evidence.why: Runtime integration shows the audit mechanism is wired into agent dispatch execution.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations become BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that prevent PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Shows enforcement mechanism blocks progress when agents violate project mutation boundary.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' writes untracked-mutation.txt and verifies blocked status.
evidence.why: Test evidence that git ls-files approach catches untracked file creation.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs' writes .env and verifies violation.
evidence.why: Test evidence that boundary catches gitignored files when outside standard exclusion patterns.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' verifies walkFiles fallback and artifact exclusion.
evidence.why: Test evidence that non-git fallback walks work and exclude artifact root correctly.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states 'dispatch会在运行前后审计项目文件边界，任何越界改动都会变成readiness blocker' (dispatch audits before/after, violations become blockers).
evidence.why: Product requirement documentation confirms boundary audit is a designed feature, not accidental.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' shows boundary enforcement is independent of agent success.
evidence.why: Confirms that boundary audit is not bypassed by successful agent results.

evidence.summary: Untracked .yolo/stress/ directory exists but no evidence files are present showing stress test results.
evidence.why: Suggests stress testing may be planned or attempted but no completed evidence exists.

assumptions: The .yolo/stress/ directory indicates stress testing intent, but no completed stress test artifacts exist to validate real Claude -p agent behavior.
The current dirty working tree may include modifications that are not yet committed, but the status_quo claim specifically references stress testing gaps rather than general tree state.
Tests use mock agents (fakeAgentOutput) rather than real Claude -p provider invocations, so actual agent tool behavior is not covered by existing tests.
risks: Real Claude -p agents may access tools or behave differently than mocked agents in tests, potentially bypassing boundary checks in ways not covered by unit tests.
git ls-files -co --exclude-standard may not catch all mutation types (e.g., staged but not committed changes, submodule mutations).
File walk fallback may be slow on large projects and could timeout before completing boundary audit.
Excluded directory list (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) may not cover all build artifact patterns across different project types.
missing: Stress test evidence from real Claude -p agents (codex, claude) using the dispatch system with full tool access.
Evidence showing boundary audit performance and correctness on large project trees.
Integration test evidence showing boundary behavior with staged git changes and submodules.
Evidence showing boundary behavior when agents intentionally try to mutate project files vs accidental mutation.

result.notes: Core boundary audit implementation exists with git and fallback coverage. Tests cover key scenarios but use mocked agents. Missing stress test evidence with real Claude -p providers is the primary gap between status_quo and desired_outcome.
